title: Oxybasis frigida
taxonomy: Oxybasis frigida is a species of flowering plant in the family Amaranthaceae, commonly known as the cold water spinach or cold spinach. It is native to Europe and Asia but has been naturalized in other parts of the world. This plant is an annual or biennial herb that grows up to 1 meter in height and has green leaves and small white flowers. Oxybasis frigida is often found in wet environments and is edible, making it a popular ingredient in various cuisines. Its classification is further divided into two subspecies, Oxybasis frigida subsp. frigida and Oxybasis frigida subsp. montana.

intro: Oxybasis frigida, also known as frostweed or ice plant, is a perennial plant species belonging to the family Amaranthaceae.
